event_description: S&P 500 index addition announcement: when S&P Dow Jones Indices announces a stock will join the S&P 500 (after market hours), buy at the next open and hold for 5 trading days. The forced buying by passive index funds drives significant positive abnormal returns.
causal_mechanism: Passive index funds (>$5T in S&P 500 trackers) must buy the newly added stock before the effective date to minimize tracking error. These funds are price-insensitive buyers. Forced demand creates a predictable price pressure. Arbitrageurs front-run index fund buying, amplifying the initial pop. Result: stock rises abnormally from announcement to ~effective date.

out_of_sample_split.verdict: PASS - effect holds in both temporal windows

confounders.thematic_momentum: TSLA, MRNA, SMCI, PLTR had sector tailwinds. Non-thematic additions still show +3-7% 1d.
confounders.market_regime: Bull (2020-2021), bear (2022), AI bull (2023-2024). Effect holds across regimes.
confounders.announcement_window_length: Quarterly: ~17 days. Off-cycle: 3-7 days. Shorter windows = less total drift.
confounders.arbitrage_front_running: G&S (2025) claim effect gone by 2020. Our 2020-2024 data contradicts this.

market_regime_note: Effect holds in bull (2020-2021, 2023-2024) and bear (2022) markets. 1-5d most reliable.
literature_reference: Shleifer (1986) JF; Harris & Gurel (1986) JF; Chen et al. (2004) JF: +6% CAR announcement-to-effective. Greenwood & Sammon (2025) JF: 'The Disappearing Index Effect' - <1% by 2010-2020. OWN DATA: Effect clearly present in 2020-2024 despite G&S finding.
survivorship_bias_note: SBNY (Signature Bank, bankrupt March 2023) and CTLT (Catalent, acquired 2024) missing from dataset. LOW RISK: neither failed within 5 days of announcement - the 5-day hold period is unaffected. Dataset is otherwise exhaustive for all S&P 500 additions 2020-2024.
selection_bias_note: Exhaustive dataset - all S&P 500 announcements between Sept 2020 and Sept 2024 where data was available. NOT cherry-picked based on outcomes. Missing events (2) due to delistings/acquisitions, not selection bias. Excludes spinoffs (different mechanism) where possible.
confounder_warnings: Missing confounders: ['broad_market_direction', 'vix_level', 'sector_trend', 'survivorship_bias', 'selection_bias', 'event_timing']. All confounders from methodology.json should be recorded, even if the value is 'unknown' or 'not applicable'.

live_validation_march_2026.note: 5-day window already passed (Mar 9-13). All 4 additions positive, consistent with hypothesis. Effective date Mar 23.

event_description: When 2+ corporate insiders (officers/directors) make open-market stock purchases within 30 days of each other (each >$50K), the stock outperforms over the next 20 trading days. Signal identified from SEC Form 4 filing dates (public information).
causal_mechanism: Corporate insiders have material non-public information about company prospects. When multiple insiders independently buy within 30 days, it signals collective conviction about undervaluation — less likely to be idiosyncratic routine purchases or 10b5-1 plan execution. Cluster purchase confirmed via SEC Form 4 filing makes signal public. Momentum and factor investors then enter long positions, driving price discovery over the following weeks.

out_of_sample_split.note: PRE-REGISTRATION: These are literature-anchor placeholders. Real OOS split will use 2020-2021 as discovery and 2022-2024 as validation after SEC Form 4 data collection. Temporal cutoff: 2022-01-01.

confounders.broad_market_direction: mixed — cluster events span bull and bear regimes
confounders.vix_level: unknown — will analyze by regime after backtest
confounders.sector_trend: diverse sectors — Form 4 data spans all sectors
confounders.survivorship_bias: SEC Form 4 captures all reporting companies at time of filing including later-delisted. yfinance may fail for delisted tickers — will track drop rate.
confounders.selection_bias: Signal tested on Form 4 filing date (public info), not actual purchase date. 2-business-day reporting deadline means signal lags purchase by 1-2 days.
confounders.event_timing: after_hours — Form 4 filings are typically submitted after market close
confounders.market_regime: to be analyzed post-backtest; will segment by VIX at cluster date

market_regime_note: Cluster purchases span multiple market regimes (2020 COVID crash, 2021 bull, 2022 bear). Effect may be regime-dependent — insider buying into a crash may outperform more than buying during a calm bull market. Will analyze subsets by VIX regime post-backtest.
regime_note: Signal may be stronger during elevated-VIX periods when insider conviction signals more information
literature_reference: Lakonishok & Lee (2001, JF): insider purchases predict 3-month outperformance. Alldredge & Blank (2017): cluster purchases yield 2.1% monthly abnormal vs 1.2% solitary. Kang, Kim & Wang (2018): cluster purchases yield 3.8% abnormal over 21 trading days. Finance Letters (2024): suggests signal may only persist in illiquid small-caps.
survivorship_bias_note: Using SEC Form 4 historical bulk data which captures all companies at time of filing including later-delisted firms. No forward-looking survivorship bias in event identification. However, yfinance may fail to fetch price data for delisted tickers — will note data failure rate and check if drops are non-random (i.e., are failing tickers systematically different from succeeding ones).
selection_bias_note: Insider purchases are voluntary but legally required within 2 business days of the transaction. Signal tested on Form 4 filing date (public information), not actual purchase date. This simulates realistic tradeable timing. Key selection concern: insiders may be more likely to report promptly when the purchase was beneficial (winner's disclosure bias). Will test whether late filers (>2 days) have different outcomes than prompt filers.
